semiconductors.summary: Semis staged a broad relief rally with SOXX +5.28%, led by memory names (MU +11.3%, SNDK +12.9%) and AMD (+7.8%) on the Helios AI system launch. GOOG and INTC earnings tomorrow are the near-term regime markers, while the overarching sentiment remains cautious ahead of hyperscaler capex guidance season.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically fragile — supply-chain demand intact, but capex sustainability is the overhang.
semiconductors.key_development: Tuesday's semis surge was a bounce, not a trend change; GOOG earnings (Jul 23) will be the first demand-side signal from a hyperscaler.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is divided: GOOG sits at invalidation ahead of earnings, MSFT is hampered by OpenAI concentration, and META is hard-blocked. AMZN's AWS acceleration narrative is the most constructive, but all eyes are on the Q2 earnings season to validate or challenge AI capex. PLTR remains expensive and drifting.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex bets are high, but earnings must now prove the ROI.
big_tech.key_development: Google reports tomorrow; a capex guidance cut would be the first crack in the hyperscaler demand thesis.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment for AI optics — sister relationship to LITE, directly benefiting from the same CPO/OCS demand cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Q3 earnings confirm > $400M CPO backlog and COHR trades below its 50-day in sympathy.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and data-center networking chip supplier, direct beneficiary of hyperscaler capex and the AI networking buildout — adjacent to AVGO and NVDA.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit if hyperscaler Q2 capex guidance comes in above consensus and MRVL pulls back to its 200-day.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: High-speed datacenter switching for AI clusters — tied to the same optical/networking capex as LITE and NOK.
interconnected.entry_note: Worth adding on a 10-15% pullback from current levels if AI networking spending is confirmed in Big Tech earnings.

active_risks: US-Iran conflict: 11th consecutive night of US strikes on Iran; Iran closed Strait of Hormuz after US airstrikes, and Houthis threatened a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, threatening dual chokepoint closure (Hormuz + Red Sea).
US-China AI tech tensions: US Treasury Secretary Bessent threatened sanctions on Chinese AI labs over alleged IP theft from American models; potential addition of Chinese AI firms to Entity List.
Trump trade tariffs: New 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods announced, and hints of broader tariff regime to rebuild protectionist agenda.
MAS policy risk: Singapore monetary authority's quarterly review due by July 31; further S$NEER tightening could intensify NIM compression for SG banks.
geopolitical.market_impact: WTI crude rallied to $85.28 as dual chokepoint risk escalates; a closure of both the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea could push oil above $100 (per Kpler). Singapore banks are directly exposed to higher energy costs and trade disruption, while semis are indirectly affected via energy costs and supply-chain fears. Gold rose 2.1% on safe-haven demand.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Houthis announced a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, and two Saudi crude tankers turned around in the Red Sea. US identified troops killed in Iran attack and launched retaliatory strikes. US Treasury Secretary threatened AI-related sanctions on China.

scenarios.base: Hormuz remains disrupted but not fully closed; WTI stays in the $80-90 range. Semis continue to trade around their 50-day averages, with earnings-driven volatility. Fed holds on July 29, and Big Tech capex guidance remains steady.
scenarios.optimistic: Ceasefire talks progress, WTI retreats below $80, and Fed remains on hold. Semis rally on receding energy risk, and hyperscaler capex guidance surprises to the upside, lifting the AI infrastructure complex.
scenarios.pessimistic: Dual chokepoint closure drives WTI above $100; Fed forced to hike on July 29. Semis sell off sharply as energy costs threaten AI ROI, and capex guidance cuts from Google/Microsoft trigger a broad tech rout.
scenarios.wildcard: US sanctions Chinese AI labs, escalating tech war; China retaliates with rare earth export bans. Memory and semiconductor supply chains are disrupted, and the AI capex thesis breaks globally.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold existing positions; do not add new capital until the July 29 FOMC and Big Tech earnings season provide clarity on rate direction and capex sustainability. The extreme concentration in memory/semis (80% of portfolio) is the single biggest risk.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — wait for earnings and FOMC clarity
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 4 of 32 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; the portfolio is heavily concentrated in semis and energy-exposed names, making it vulnerable to both an oil spike and a capex disappointment.
